2606.10369.title: PADD: Path-Aligned Decompression Distillation for Non-Router Teacher to Guide MoE Student Learning
2606.10369.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) continue to scale, it becomes increasingly challenging to grow model capacity under fixed computation budgets. We propose Path-Aligned Decompression Distillation (PADD), a framework for distilling knowledge from dense teachers without explicit routing into mixture-of-experts (MoE) students while learning high-quality routing policies. PADD organizes knowledge distillation into four stages in two phases: an initialization phase (Stage I) that builds diverse functionality in the student's experts through teacher neuron clustering and student-expert warmup, and a training phase (Stages II--IV) that integrates online adaptive distillation, path-refined policy optimization, and reward-augmented load balancing in a single training pipeline. Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that PADD yields substantial gains over strong baselines at the same inference cost and that the MoE student can match or surpass its dense teacher. They also demonstrate effective teacher-to-student knowledge distillation and stable routing behavior.
2606.10369.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）持續擴展，在固定計算預算下增長模型容量變得越來越具挑戰性。我們提出了路徑對齊解壓蒸餾（PADD），這是一個從密集教師中提取知識的框架，無需明確路由到混合專家（MoE）學生，同時學習高質量的路由策略。PADD將知識蒸餾組織成四個階段，分為兩個階段：初始化階段（第一階段）通過教師神經元聚類和學生-專家預熱來建立學生專家的多樣化功能，以及訓練階段（第二至第四階段）在單一訓練管道中整合在線自適應蒸餾、路徑精煉策略優化和增強獎勵的負載平衡。在數學推理基準上的實驗表明，PADD在相同推理成本下相較於強基準獲得了顯著增益，並且MoE學生可以匹配或超越其密集教師。這些實驗還展示了有效的教師到學生的知識蒸餾和穩定的路由行為。
2606.10369.author: Xinyue Peng et.al.
2606.10369.authors: Xinyue Peng, Yi Qian, Jiaojiao Lin, Wenjian Shao, Yanming Liu
